title: RAG, Long Context, Fine-Tuning, Memory, and Architecture Evolution · Transfer Build
summary: An independent team receives a different corpus, query mix, relevance policy, language set, access model, latency budget, harm profile, and evidence duty and transfers rag, long context, fine-tuning, memory, and architecture evolution into a new evidence-based architecture and migration record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of evidence-based architecture and migration record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit t…
objectives: Compare search, RAG, full-context, cached-context, and hierarchical approaches on corpus size, freshness, authorization, position, latency, cost, citations, and quality.
Use retrieval for external mutable evidence and fine-tuning for evaluated behavior or domain adaptation while testing combinations and update cost.
Evaluate corrective retrieval, self-reflection, verification, query rewriting, and fallback against deterministic stage evidence and false confidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Rag Long Context Comparison
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab RAG engineering team handles case 5196 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare RAG, long context, fine-tuning, memory, graph, and self-corrective designs before migration; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Architecture Migration Agility
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-architecture-migration-agility-c6ujdo" that uses case 9966 to compare RAG, long context, fine-tuning, memory, graph, and self-corrective designs before migration. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Architecture Migration Agility.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Architecture Migration Agility into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Architecture Migration Agility, keep Rag Architecture Migration Agility connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-architecture-migration-agility-c6ujdo
# Competency: rag-architecture-migration-agility.
# Case 9966: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence.
# Changed case: change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence.
def evidence_rag_architecture_migration_agility_c6ujdo(baseline_score: float = 0.74, candidate_score: float = 0.83, migration_cost: int = 40, benefit_units: int = 90, long_context_tested: bool = True, rollback_possible: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_c6ujdo = "c6ujdo-odju6c-9966"
    assert evidence_variant_c6ujdo
    evidence_axes_c6ujdo = (
        "corpusc6ujdo",
        "queryodju6c",
        "tenant9966",
        "indexc6ujdo9966",
        "promptodju6c9966",
        "evaluation9966c6ujdo",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_c6ujdo)) == 6
    if not 0.0 <= baseline_score <= 1.0 or not 0.0 <= candidate_score <= 1.0:
        return False, 0.0, "score-range"
    if migration_cost < 0 or benefit_units < 0:
        return False, 0.0, "change-cost"
    if not long_context_tested:
        return False, candidate_score, "architecture-comparison"
    if candidate_score <= baseline_score or benefit_units <= migration_cost:
        return False, candidate_score - baseline_score, "complexity-not-earned"
    if not rollback_possible:
        return False, candidate_score, "migration-safety"
    improvement = candidate_score - baseline_score
    assert improvement > 0.0
    return True, round(improvement, 4), "earned-evolution"
